summary: Label printing app with barcode scanning functionality. User is iterating on label layout with barcode width adjustments and duplicate serial validation.
architectural_notes: 1st and 2nd scanned serials must be unique; duplicate detection required at scan time with audio feedback
known_issues: Barcode not fully fitting within label boundaries (width needs adjustment)

new_feature_requests.summary: Adjust barcode width so barcode fits fully within label boundaries
Add barcode width adjustment option in label settings for user fine-tuning
When scanning 2nd barcode, show error and play sound notification if serial matches 1st barcode (serials must be unique)

Reduce the default barcode width in the CPCL generator so that both barcodes fit fully within the printable area of the label without being clipped on either side. Reference cpclGenerator.ts and cpclLayoutAdjustments.ts to tighten the width calculation and enforce a safe maximum width based on label dimensions minus left/right margins.

Add a 'Barcode Width' numeric input control in the Label Settings tab (LabelSettingsTab.tsx) that allows the user to fine-tune the barcode width value. This setting must be persisted in the label settings store (labelSettingsStore.ts) alongside existing barcode layout fields, and must be applied when generating CPCL commands in cpclGenerator.ts.

In the two-step scan workflow (ScanPrintTab.tsx), when the user scans the 2nd barcode, compare its normalized serial value against the already-scanned 1st serial. If they are identical, display a visible error message stating the two serials must be unique, play the error sound using the existing sound system (soundSystem.ts), and clear the 2nd input so the user can re-scan. Do not proceed to print when this duplicate condition is detected.

projectName: Adjust barcode width to fit label, add width setting, and enforce unique serial validation
